sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — SNES / Kirby Super Star
sources.notes: Confirms sub-pixel system: X Position (2 bytes at 0988), X Sub-position (1 byte at 0A7D), Y Position, Y Sub-position. X/Y velocity stored at 16BF and 0BEA. Specific numerical values not documented in reviewed page.

sources.name: Kirby's Adventure NES (predecessor)
sources.notes: Reference: KA walk speed 332 sub-px/frame = 1.297 px/f (256 sub-px/pixel). KSS likely uses different values.

notes: Kirby Super Star is an SNES anthology: 8 separate game modes in one cartridge.
Sub-pixel system confirmed by TASVideos: 2-byte position + 1-byte sub-position for X and Y (256 sub-pixels/pixel).
Core Kirby mechanics: walk, run, float (unlimited), inhale, swallow (copy ability), throw enemy.
25 COPY ABILITIES — the largest ability set in the series to this point.
HELPER SYSTEM: New in KSS. Kirby can give a copy ability to a CPU or second-player partner (Helper).
Float is UNLIMITED in KSS (unlike earlier Kirby games where float had limited puffs).
Walk and float speeds not confirmed from reviewed sources. Values below from KA predecessor context + community.

subpixel_system.description: Confirmed by TASVideos: separate X/Y Position (2-byte) and X/Y Sub-position (1-byte) registers.

walk_speed.notes: Approximate from gameplay. KA predecessor had 332 sub-px/frame = 1.297 px/f. KSS likely similar. VERIFY.

run_speed.notes: Approximate. Kirby runs when holding B or after accelerating. VERIFY.

acceleration.notes: Kirby has a short acceleration ramp — slides slightly on direction reversal. Not instant like Mega Man.

float._note: Unlimited float — the core mobility tool. Kirby can float indefinitely by repeatedly pressing jump.
float.method: Each jump press causes Kirby to puff up and gain height
float.horizontal_speed_maintained: Yes — Kirby maintains horizontal momentum while floating

exhale.description: Pressing B while floating causes Kirby to exhale air — a star projectile that damages enemies.
exhale.notes: Exhaling ends the float. Must land or re-puff to float again.

notes: Unlimited float changes the difficulty model entirely from Mario-style games. Vertical challenge isn't about 'can you reach this platform' but 'can you navigate while floating.'
Floating Kirby is vulnerable — enemies can hit him mid-float and knock him down.
The exhale attack creates a tradeoff: attack vs. continue floating.

inhale_system.description: Kirby opens his mouth and holds it to create a suction force. Enemies and items get pulled toward him.

swallow.description: Swallow the inhaled enemy/item to copy its ability (if it has one).
swallow.notes: Swallowing is how copy abilities are obtained.

spit.description: Press B to spit the inhaled enemy as a projectile — the star spit. Deals damage to other enemies on contact.

copy_abilities.notes: 25 copy abilities in KSS — the largest set in Kirby history at this point. Each fundamentally changes Kirby's attack style.

movement_affecting_abilities.jet: Jet: Charge up a jet burst for high-speed flight. The fastest horizontal movement in the game.
movement_affecting_abilities.wheel: Wheel: Kirby becomes a wheel for extremely fast ground movement.
movement_affecting_abilities.wing: Wing: Kirby gets wings for sustained directional flight — faster and more controlled than puffed float.
movement_affecting_abilities.suplex: Suplex: Grab-and-throw mechanics — repositions enemies rather than projectile damage.
movement_affecting_abilities.helper: Any ability can be given to a Helper (CPU or second player partner).

helper_system.description: New in KSS: Kirby can throw a copy ability to create a Helper — a CPU-controlled partner that fights alongside him.
helper_system.helper_creation: Hold B while holding a copy ability → ability bubble appears → CPU takes control as Helper.
helper_system.second_player: In 2-player mode, Player 2 controls the Helper directly.
notes: The Helper changes the game from single-player to asymmetric co-op.
Helpers have the same moveset as the ability type they represent.
Helper can absorb hits to save Kirby — essentially a second HP bar.
Different abilities create different Helper archetypes (Sword = melee, Laser = ranged, etc.).

game_modes._note: KSS is an anthology. Physics core is the same across modes but each mode has a different structure.
game_modes.spring_breeze: Abbreviated remake of Kirby's Dream Land 1. Linear stages.
game_modes.gourmet_race: Race Kirby against King Dedede through 3 stages. Time + food collection.
game_modes.revenge_of_meta_knight: Linear aerial battle stages on the Halberd airship.
game_modes.milky_way_wishes: Non-linear star-based world selection. No copy abilities from enemies — must find Copy Essences.
game_modes.the_arena: Boss rush — fight all bosses consecutively.

kirby.notes: Kirby is round — hitbox is approximately circular with radius ≈ 8 pixels. VERIFY.

design_notes_for_giants_drink: The anthology structure of KSS is a design statement: 'Here are 8 different games using the same physics engine and character.' Each mode has a different structure (linear, non-linear, boss rush, minigame) but the underlying movement is shared. This is exactly the 'paradigm swap' philosophy in Giants Drink.

The unlimited float fundamentally changes the difficulty design contract. In Kirby games, you never need to worry about 'can I reach that platform' — the float guarantees it. Difficulty comes from navigating while being bombarded by enemies, not from precision jumping.

The 25 copy abilities create a game with enormous mechanical variety within a single physics framework. Each ability is essentially a different weapon loadout that changes your approach to every enemy. This is the 'same body, different tools' design philosophy.

The Helper system is early cooperative gaming design done thoughtfully: two players with asymmetric capabilities (Kirby hosts the body/float; Helper provides the combat ability) create interesting coordination dynamics.
KSS's Milky Way Wishes (no abilities from enemies) forces players to think about which Copy Essence locations they've found — it's light resource management layered on top of the platforming.
